prompt: If your writing style were a material (wood, brass, cotton, glass, concrete, etc.), which would it be and why?
response: If my writing style were a material, it would be glass. Like glass, my writing aims to be clear and transparent, allowing ideas to shine through without distortion. It’s crafted to be smooth and polished, yet strong enough to hold complex thoughts together. At the same time, glass can be shaped into many forms—just as I adapt my tone and structure to fit different topics and audiences. Plus, like glass, my style strives to reflect light from different perspectives, helping readers see things in a new, illuminating way.
